title: update_contact_fully: debug_assert\! doesn't protect release from spurious state bump
description: If a future caller passes (None, None) to update_contact_fully in a release build, the debug_assert\! is compiled out, the function runs the existence SELECT, skips both UPDATE branches, then unconditionally bumps the ChatContact state-token — a spurious JMAP push notification with no field change. The current caller (contact_set Phase 2b) correctly guards with 'if blocked.is_none() && display_name.is_none()' before calling, so this cannot be triggered today. But the function signature and docstring document this as accepted behavior ('calling with both None is a no-op (existence is checked and ChatContact state is bumped, but no field changes)') rather than fixing it. A future caller that does not know this contract will silently trigger unnecessary client refreshes.
close_reason: Replaced debug_assert! with runtime Err guard; added store unit test to verify precondition enforcement in both debug and release builds
